Related Texts CONTINUAL EXPOSITION TO THE FUTURE POSITION Allah (88) says: EV (OSI Sai 5058 Ok

Tbn ‘Umar (#) said that Allah’s Messenger (4) said:

<When one of you dies, he is shown his (future) position morning and evening. If he is from the people of Jannah, (he is shown his place) in Jannah, and if he is from the people of the Fire, (he is shown his place) in the Fire; and each is told, “This will be your place when Allah raises you on the Day of Resurrection.”> * ;

SCREAMS OF AGONY Ibn Mas‘tid (#) said that Allah’s Messenger (4) said: <Verily, the dead are punished in their graves, and

even the animals hear their screaming. *

2 Al-Bukhari and Muslim.

3 Recorded by Abi Nu‘aym and at-Tabarani in al-Kabir; verified to be authentic by al-Albani (Sahih ul-Jami‘ no. 1965).

Zayd Bin Thabit () reported that Allah’s Messenger (88) said:

DARKNESS IN THE GRAVE

Abi Hurayrah and Anas (:) reported that Allah’s Messenger (4%)

‘Indeed, these graves engulf their dwellers with darkness; and verily Allah illuminates them (for the believers) because of my salah (supplication) for ‘them. ?

EATING FROM THE TREES OF JANNAH Ka’b Bin Malik narrated that Allah’s Messenger (&8) said:

| Muslim and Ahmad.

2 Muslim and others.

CHAPTER 7 THE PROPHETS AND THE MARTYRS

The prophets, the martyrs, and the most righteous believers have a special well-earned status in the hereafter. This applies to al-Barzakh as well. The prophets’ bodies are preserved from decay, and they live a special type of life in which they continuously pray to their Lord (3). The martyrs’ souls are with the other believers’ souls in Jannah, but their souls are within green birds, and they are closer to Allah, being immediately under the Throne.

Preservation of the Prophets’ Bodies

The earth does not consume the bodies of the Prophets. Aws Bin Aws (5) narrated that Allah’s Messenger (&) said:

<Indeed, among the best of all of your days is the day of Jumu‘ah (Friday). On such a day, Adam was created, he died, on it the blow (of the Sur or trumpet of the Doom's Hour) will be sounded, and on it is the great shock (when all those on earth will die following the blow of the Sar); so say in plenty your salah upon me on Friday, for indeed your salah will be presented to me. Indeed, Allah has prohibited the earth from eating up the bodies of prophets. '

1 Recorded by Ahmad, Abi Dawid and others; verified to be authentic by al-Albant (Sahih ul-Jami‘ no. 2212).

The Prophets' Life in Their Graves

The Prophets live and pray in their graves. However, their life is not the life of this world, but is a life from al-Barzakh. What distinguishes it from the life of the other believers is that the latter sleep in their graves, whereas the prophets are granted the ability and merit to continue to pray even after death. Anas Bin Malik (5) reported that Allah’s Messenger (2) said:

<The prophets are alive in their graves; and they are praying (therein).> |

Anas (4%) also reported that the Messenger (48) said:

Some scholars, such as al-Manawi, believe that this hadith refers to the original linguistic meaning of the prayer, “It means that he was making du‘@, praising Allah, and mentioning Him.” * Others, such as al-Qurtubi, believe that it refers to the actual shart (legislated) prayer.

Allah (88) appoints an angel at the grave of Allah’s Messenger (88) to inform him, by name, of each one who says the salah upon him. Abi Bakr (<8) reported that Allah’s Messenger (#) said:

1 Recorded by Abi Y‘ala, al-Bazzar and others; verified to be authentic by al-Albant (Sahih ul-Jami’ no. 2790).

Isr@: The Prophet's (@) miraculous night trip from Makkah to Jerusalem. Muslim and others.

Life in Al-Barzakh 51 The Prophets and the Martyrs

<Say the salah upon me frequently, because Allah has appointed for me an angel who stays by my grave. Whenever one of my Ummah (Nation or followers) says the salah upon me, that angel says to me, “OO Muhammad, such and such person said the salah upon you at this moment.”> '

Also, Abii Mas‘tid al-Ansgari (<8) reported that the Messenger (4) also said:

<Say the salah upon me plentifully on al-Jumu‘ah (Friday), for no one says the salah upon me on Friday but his salah is presented to me. ”

Malik Bin Sa‘sa‘ah narrated that Allah’s Messenger () described to the sahabah his Night Journey (al-Isr@ and al-Mi'raj) as follows:

<While I was lying at al-Hatim *, someone suddenly came to me and cut my body open from here to here * and took out my heart. A gold washbow!l full of Iman (faith) was then brought to me. My heart was washed with Zamzam’s water and filled with Iman; it was then returned to its original place. Before me was brought an animal smaller than a mule and larger than a donkey, white in color, called al-Buragq. (It travels so fast that) it places its footstep

1 Recorded by ad-Daylami; verified to be hasan by al-Albant (Sahih ul-Jami‘ no.

2 Recorded by al-Hakim and al-Bayhaqj; verified to be authentic by al-Albani (Sahih ul-Jami’ no. 1208).

3. The wall of al-Ka‘bah nearest to the black stone. It is so called because people crowd around it.

4 From his throat to his navel.

at the end of its eyesight. I was carried on it, and Jibril led me till he reached the lowest heaven.

When he requested admission, he was asked, “Who is it?” He said, “Jibril.’” He was asked, “Who accompanies you?” He replied, “Muhammad.” He was asked, “Has he been summoned?” He said, “Yes.” It was then said, “He is welcome; what an excellent visit his is!” The gate was opened. When I entered, I saw Adam (8). Jibril said, “This is your father, Adam; so give him salam (greeting with peace).” I gave him salam. He returned my salam and said, “Welcome, righteous Prophet and righteous son.”

Jibril then ascended with me until we reached the second heaven. He requested admission, and was asked, “Who is it?” He said, “Jibril.” He was asked, “Who accompanies you?” He replied, “Muhammad.” He was asked, “Has he been summoned?” He said, “Yes.” It was then said, “He is welcome; what an excellent visit his is!” The gate was opened. When I entered, I saw Yahya (John) and Isa (Jesus), who are cousins. Jibril said, “These are Yahya and ‘Isa; give them salam.” I gave them salam. They returned my salam and said, “Welcome, righteous brother and righteous prophet.”

Jibril then ascended with me to the third heaven. He requested admission, and was asked, “Who is it?” He said, “Jibril.” He was asked, “Who accompanies you?” He replied, “Muhammad.” He was asked, “Has he been summoned?” He said, “Yes.’”’ It was then said, “He is welcome; what an excellent visit his is!” The gate was opened. When I entered I saw Yisuf. Jibril said, “This is Yasuf; give him salam.” I gave him salam. He returned my salam and said, “Welcome, righteous brother and_ righteous prophet.”

Jibril then ascended with me to the fourth heaven. He requested admission, and was asked,

Life in Al-Barzakh 53 The Prophets and the Martyrs

“Who is it?” He said, “Jibril.” He was asked, “Who accompanies you?” He replied, “Muhammad.” He was asked, “Has he been summoned?” He said, “Yes.”’ It was then said, “He is welcome; what an excellent visit his is!” The gate was opened. When I entered, I saw Idris. Jibril said, “This is Idris; give him salam.” I gave him salam. He returned my salam and said, “Welcome, righteous brother and righteous prophet.”

Jibril then ascended with me to the fifth heaven. He requested admission, and was asked, “Who is it?” He said, “Jibril.”” He was asked, “Who accompanies you?” He replied, “Muhammad.” He was asked, “Has he been summoned?” He said, “Yes.” It was then said, “He is welcome; what an excellent visit his is!” When I entered I saw Haran (Aaron). Jibril said, “This is Haran; give him salam.” I gave him salam. He returned my salam and said, “Welcome, righteous brother and righteous prophet.”

Jibril then ascended with me to the sixth heaven. He requested admission, and was asked, “Who is it?” He said, “Jibril.” He was asked, “Who accompanies you?” He replied, “Muhammad.” He was asked, “Has he been summoned?” He said, “Yes.” It was then said, “He is welcome; what an excellent visit his is!” When I entered I saw Misa (Moses). Jibril said, “This is Misa; give him salam.” I gave him salam. He returned my salam and said, “Welcome, righteous brother and righteous prophet.” After I passed on, he began crying; he was asked, “What makes you cry?” He said, “I am crying because a young man, sent after me (with the Message from Allah), has followers who will enter Jannah in greater numbers than my followers.”

Dalil-Dalil Terkait PENUNJUKAN TERUS-MENERUS TERHADAP KEDUDUKAN DI MASA DEPAN Allah (88) berfirman: EV (OSI Sai 5058 Ok

Ibnu 'Umar (#) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah (4) bersabda:

<Apabila salah seorang dari kalian meninggal, ia ditunjukkan kedudukannya (di masa depan) pada pagi dan petang hari. Jika ia termasuk penghuni Jannah, (ia ditunjukkan tempatnya) di Jannah, dan jika ia termasuk penghuni Neraka, (ia ditunjukkan tempatnya) di Neraka; dan masing-masing diberitahu, "Ini akan menjadi tempatmu ketika Allah membangkitkanmu pada Hari Kiamat."> * ;

JERITAN KESAKITAN Ibnu Mas'ud (#) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah (4) bersabda: <Sesungguhnya orang-orang yang telah meninggal disiksa di dalam kubur mereka, dan

bahkan hewan-hewan pun mendengar jeritan mereka.> *

2 Al-Bukhari dan Muslim.

3 Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Nu'aym dan at-Tabarani dalam al-Kabir; diverifikasi sahih oleh al-Albani (Sahih ul-Jami' no. 1965).

Zayd bin Tsabit () meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah (88) bersabda:

KEGELAPAN DALAM KUBUR

Abu Hurayrah dan Anas (:) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah (4%)

'Sesungguhnya kubur-kubur ini menyelimuti penghuninya dengan kegelapan; dan sungguh Allah meneranginya (bagi orang-orang beriman) karena salah (du'a) ku untuk mereka.'

MAKAN DARI POHON-POHON JANNAH Ka'b bin Malik meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah (&8) bersabda:

| Muslim dan Ahmad.

2 Muslim dan lainnya.

BAB 7 PARA NABI DAN PARA SYUHADA

Para nabi, para syuhada, dan orang-orang beriman yang paling saleh memiliki kedudukan istimewa yang pantas mereka terima di akhirat. Hal ini juga berlaku di al-Barzakh. Tubuh para nabi terpelihara dari pembusukan, dan mereka menjalani kehidupan khusus di mana mereka terus-menerus salah kepada Rabb mereka (3). Ruh para syuhada bersama ruh orang-orang beriman lainnya di Jannah, tetapi ruh mereka berada di dalam burung-burung hijau, dan mereka lebih dekat kepada Allah, berada tepat di bawah 'Arsy.

Terpeliharanya Tubuh Para Nabi

Bumi tidak memakan tubuh para nabi. Aws bin Aws (5) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah (&) bersabda:

<Sesungguhnya di antara hari-hari kalian yang paling utama adalah hari Jumu'ah (Jumat). Pada hari itulah Adam diciptakan, pada hari itu pula ia wafat, pada hari itu pula tiupan (sangkakala Hari Kiamat) akan dibunyikan, dan pada hari itu pula terjadi guncangan besar (ketika semua yang ada di bumi akan mati setelah tiupan sangkakala); maka perbanyaklah salah kalian kepadaku pada hari Jumat, karena sesungguhnya salah kalian akan disampaikan kepadaku. Sesungguhnya Allah telah mengharamkan bumi untuk memakan tubuh para nabi.>

1 Diriwayatkan oleh Ahmad, Abu Dawud dan lainnya; diverifikasi sahih oleh al-Albani (Sahih ul-Jami' no. 2212).

Kehidupan Para Nabi di Dalam Kubur Mereka

Para nabi hidup dan salah di dalam kubur mereka. Namun, kehidupan mereka bukanlah kehidupan dunia ini, melainkan kehidupan al-Barzakh. Yang membedakannya dari kehidupan orang-orang beriman lainnya adalah bahwa yang terakhir ini tidur di dalam kubur mereka, sedangkan para nabi diberikan kemampuan dan keutamaan untuk terus salah bahkan setelah wafat. Anas bin Malik (5) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah (2) bersabda:

<Para nabi hidup di dalam kubur mereka; dan mereka salah (di dalamnya).> |

Anas (4%) juga meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah (48) bersabda:

Sebagian ulama, seperti al-Manawi, berpendapat bahwa hadith ini merujuk pada makna linguistik asli dari salah, "Maknanya adalah bahwa beliau berdu'a, memuji Allah, dan menyebut-Nya." * Ulama lainnya, seperti al-Qurtubi, berpendapat bahwa itu merujuk pada salah syar'i (yang disyariatkan) yang sesungguhnya.

Allah (88) menugaskan seorang malaikat di kubur Rasulullah (88) untuk memberitahukan kepadanya, dengan menyebut nama, setiap orang yang mengucapkan salah kepadanya. Abu Bakr (<8) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah (#) bersabda:

1 Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Ya'la, al-Bazzar dan lainnya; diverifikasi sahih oleh al-Albani (Sahih ul-Jami' no. 2790).

Isra': Perjalanan malam Nabi (@) yang penuh mukjizat dari Makkah ke Yerusalem. Muslim dan lainnya.

Kehidupan di Al-Barzakh 51 Para Nabi dan Para Syuhada

<Perbanyaklah salah kepadaku, karena Allah telah menugaskan seorang malaikat untukku yang berada di kuburku. Setiap kali seorang dari umatku mengucapkan salah kepadaku, malaikat itu berkata kepadaku, "Wahai Muhammad, si fulan mengucapkan salah kepadamu pada saat ini."> '

Juga, Abu Mas'ud al-Anshari (<8) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah (4) juga bersabda:

<Perbanyaklah salah kepadaku pada hari al-Jumu'ah (Jumat), karena tidaklah seseorang mengucapkan salah kepadaku pada hari Jumat melainkan salahnya disampaikan kepadaku."

Malik bin Sa'sa'ah meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah () menceritakan kepada para sahabah tentang Perjalanan Malamnya (al-Isra' dan al-Mi'raj) sebagai berikut:

<Ketika aku sedang berbaring di al-Hatim *, seseorang tiba-tiba datang kepadaku dan membelah tubuhku dari sini ke sini * lalu mengeluarkan hatiku. Sebuah bejana emas yang penuh dengan iman kemudian dibawakan kepadaku. Hatiku dicuci dengan air Zamzam dan diisi dengan iman; kemudian dikembalikan ke tempatnya semula. Di hadapanku dibawakan seekor hewan yang lebih kecil dari bagal dan lebih besar dari keledai, berwarna putih, bernama al-Buraq. (Ia melaju begitu cepat sehingga) ia meletakkan langkahnya

1 Diriwayatkan oleh ad-Daylami; diverifikasi hasan oleh al-Albani (Sahih ul-Jami' no.

2 Diriwayatkan oleh al-Hakim dan al-Bayhaqi; diverifikasi sahih oleh al-Albani (Sahih ul-Jami' no. 1208).

3. Dinding al-Ka'bah yang terdekat dengan Hajar Aswad. Dinamakan demikian karena orang-orang berkerumun di sekitarnya.

4 Dari tenggorokannya hingga pusarnya.

di ujung pandangannya. Aku dinaikkan ke atasnya, dan Jibril memimpinku hingga ia mencapai langit yang paling rendah.

Ketika ia meminta izin masuk, ditanyakan, "Siapakah itu?" Ia menjawab, "Jibril." Ditanyakan, "Siapa yang bersamamu?" Ia menjawab, "Muhammad." Ditanyakan, "Apakah ia telah diutus?" Ia menjawab, "Ya." Lalu dikatakan, "Selamat datang; alangkah baiknya kedatangannya!" Pintu pun dibukakan. Ketika aku masuk, aku melihat Adam (8). Jibril berkata, "Ini adalah ayahmu, Adam; ucapkanlah salam kepadanya." Aku pun mengucapkan salam kepadanya. Ia membalas salamku dan berkata, "Selamat datang, nabi yang saleh dan anak yang saleh."

Jibril kemudian naik bersamaku hingga kami mencapai langit kedua. Ia meminta izin masuk, dan ditanyakan, "Siapakah itu?" Ia menjawab, "Jibril." Ditanyakan, "Siapa yang bersamamu?" Ia menjawab, "Muhammad." Ditanyakan, "Apakah ia telah diutus?" Ia menjawab, "Ya." Lalu dikatakan, "Selamat datang; alangkah baiknya kedatangannya!" Pintu pun dibukakan. Ketika aku masuk, aku melihat Yahya (Yohanes) dan Isa (Yesus), yang merupakan sepupu. Jibril berkata, "Ini adalah Yahya dan Isa; ucapkanlah salam kepada mereka." Aku pun mengucapkan salam kepada mereka. Mereka membalas salamku dan berkata, "Selamat datang, saudara yang saleh dan nabi yang saleh."

Jibril kemudian naik bersamaku ke langit ketiga. Ia meminta izin masuk, dan ditanyakan, "Siapakah itu?" Ia menjawab, "Jibril." Ditanyakan, "Siapa yang bersamamu?" Ia menjawab, "Muhammad." Ditanyakan, "Apakah ia telah diutus?" Ia menjawab, "Ya." Lalu dikatakan, "Selamat datang; alangkah baiknya kedatangannya!" Pintu pun dibukakan. Ketika aku masuk, aku melihat Yusuf. Jibril berkata, "Ini adalah Yusuf; ucapkanlah salam kepadanya." Aku pun mengucapkan salam kepadanya. Ia membalas salamku dan berkata, "Selamat datang, saudara yang saleh dan nabi yang saleh."

Jibril kemudian naik bersamaku ke langit keempat. Ia meminta izin masuk, dan ditanyakan,

Kehidupan di Al-Barzakh 53 Para Nabi dan Para Syuhada

"Siapakah itu?" Ia menjawab, "Jibril." Ditanyakan, "Siapa yang bersamamu?" Ia menjawab, "Muhammad." Ditanyakan, "Apakah ia telah diutus?" Ia menjawab, "Ya." Lalu dikatakan, "Selamat datang; alangkah baiknya kedatangannya!" Pintu pun dibukakan. Ketika aku masuk, aku melihat Idris. Jibril berkata, "Ini adalah Idris; ucapkanlah salam kepadanya." Aku pun mengucapkan salam kepadanya. Ia membalas salamku dan berkata, "Selamat datang, saudara yang saleh dan nabi yang saleh."

Jibril kemudian naik bersamaku ke langit kelima. Ia meminta izin masuk, dan ditanyakan, "Siapakah itu?" Ia menjawab, "Jibril." Ditanyakan, "Siapa yang bersamamu?" Ia menjawab, "Muhammad." Ditanyakan, "Apakah ia telah diutus?" Ia menjawab, "Ya." Lalu dikatakan, "Selamat datang; alangkah baiknya kedatangannya!" Ketika aku masuk, aku melihat Harun (Aaron). Jibril berkata, "Ini adalah Harun; ucapkanlah salam kepadanya." Aku pun mengucapkan salam kepadanya. Ia membalas salamku dan berkata, "Selamat datang, saudara yang saleh dan nabi yang saleh."

Jibril kemudian naik bersamaku ke langit keenam. Ia meminta izin masuk, dan ditanyakan, "Siapakah itu?" Ia menjawab, "Jibril." Ditanyakan, "Siapa yang bersamamu?" Ia menjawab, "Muhammad." Ditanyakan, "Apakah ia telah diutus?" Ia menjawab, "Ya." Lalu dikatakan, "Selamat datang; alangkah baiknya kedatangannya!" Ketika aku masuk, aku melihat Musa (Musa). Jibril berkata, "Ini adalah Musa; ucapkanlah salam kepadanya." Aku pun mengucapkan salam kepadanya. Ia membalas salamku dan berkata, "Selamat datang, saudara yang saleh dan nabi yang saleh." Setelah aku berlalu, ia mulai menangis; ia ditanya, "Apa yang membuatmu menangis?" Ia menjawab, "Aku menangis karena seorang pemuda yang diutus setelahku (dengan Risalah dari Allah), memiliki pengikut yang akan memasuki Jannah dalam jumlah lebih banyak daripada pengikutku."
